method: 2 worktree Lane-V verifiers (isolating mutations) + 1 read-only completeness critic + synthesis; all Opus
rule13_sibling_found: threeway/cutover.py:144-146 pre-cursor-try strand gap -> closed by ADR-045

verdicts.defect: Defect 1: threeway/cutover.py::_teardown was a bare git-ref restore loop (EVENTS_REF-first, check=True, no try). A single ref-restore failure under refs/threeway/events.lock contention (git exit 128) raised immediately on the events ref, (a) aborting the loop so all 6 later cursor refs stayed un-restored -> half-flipped bus, and (b) propagating its own CalledProcessError in place of the real cutover cause -> original failure masked. Fix (cutover.py:78-104): best-effort-per-ref (try/except CalledProcessError, aggregate into failures[]), raise a new TeardownError CHAINED `from original` only if any ref failed, stay silent on a fully-clean restore so the caller's bare `raise` (lines 138/155) re-raises the original cause unchanged. Both run_cutover call sites (134-138, 153-155) now `except Exception as original:` and pass original to _teardown.
verdicts.mutation_run: Ran 3 isolating mutations in my worktree (/Users/hyungkoookkim/Content/.claude/worktrees/wf_be151f19-aa7-1/threeway/cutover.py), each reverted after. M1: replaced the try/best-effort body with the pre-fix bare loop (EVENTS-first, check=True, no try, no chain) -> RED: test_teardown_best_effort_continues_and_chains_original_cause (raised raw subprocess.CalledProcessError on the first/events ref instead of TeardownError; never reached the director cursor ref, so the `CUR in attempted` + `pytest.raises(TeardownError)` assertions fail). M2: dropped `from original` on the raise -> RED: test_teardown_best_effort_continues_and_chains_original_cause specifically at `assert ei.value.__cause__ is original` (None is not original) — chaining is independently load-bearing. M3: changed `if failures:` to `if True:` (always raise even on clean restore) -> RED on BOTH test_teardown_clean_restore_stays_silent (asserts no raise) AND the must-stay-green test_teardown_restores_preexisting_bus_on_failure_under_force (the always-raise masked the `injected cursor failure` cause, so the integration pin's `pytest.raises(match='injected cursor failure')` got 'teardown could not restore 0 ref(s)' instead). All mutations restored; baseline re-confirmed: 3 named pins GREEN, 34/34 cutover+refstore GREEN, 328/328 threeway GREEN.

issues.description: Pre-existing strand-state gap NOT introduced by this commit and outside Defect-1 scope: total_order()/iso_to_seq_map()/_read_iso_cursors() at cutover.py:144-146 execute after the append loop (events already written to the bus) but before/outside the cursor try-block at :148. A raise there (e.g. _read_iso_cursors OSError on seen/*.txt, or a projector/_TS regex divergence making total_order ValueError) leaves the events ref appended-over with no _teardown -> half-flipped bus. Distinct defect class from Defect 1; recommend moving lines 144-146 inside a try that calls _teardown(repo, snapshot, original), or filing a separate inventory row.

verdicts.evidence: READ: threeway/cutover.py:71-104 (TeardownError class + new _teardown), :107-168 (run_cutover, both except-as-original/_teardown/raise sites at 134-138 and 153-155, and the pre-try gap at 144-146), :57-68 (_snapshot_refs inserts EVENTS_REF first then cursor refs -> dict iteration order EVENTS-first, which is what makes the pre-fix bare loop strand the cursor refs). tests/unit/test_threeway_cutover.py:120-196 (the 3 teardown pins + the must-stay-green preexisting-bus integration test). cursor_backfill.py:31-40, 65-113 (sibling loops). RUNS (worktree python /Users/hyungkoookkim/Content/.venv/bin/python resolved threeway.cutover to the worktree path, confirmed): baseline `pytest tests/unit/test_threeway_cutover.py -q` -> 7 passed; 3 named teardown pins -> 3 passed; `tests/unit/test_threeway_cutover.py tests/unit/test_threeway_refstore.py -q` -> 34 passed; `tests/unit/ -k threeway -q` -> 328 passed, 0 failed. MUTATIONS (see mutation_run): M1 bare-loop -> best-effort pin RED; M2 drop `from original` -> __cause__ assertion RED; M3 `if True:` always-raise -> clean-restore pin AND must-stay-green preexisting-bus pin both RED. Worktree confirmed clean after restoring all mutations (git status --short shows no diff on cutover.py or tests/).

verdicts.defect: Defect 2 (dedup keys drop distinct-target revoke/supersede). Pre-fix, idempotency_key (threeway/envelope.py:97) and _request_fingerprint (threeway/refstore.py:73) both omitted the load-bearing top-level Event fields revokes_event_id/supersedes_event_id (declared envelope.py:53-54, OUTSIDE payload per envelope.py:67-69, so payload_digest does not cover them). A 2nd overseer revoke/supersede of a DIFFERENT target (identical sender/kind/payload) collided on idempotency_key in _find_verified_idempotent (refstore.py:116-117), matched the same fingerprint, and was silently returned/deduped (refstore.py:140-141) -> the 2nd target never landed and stayed effective toward quorum. Fix: both keys now append revokes/supersedes ids (envelope.py:105-106; refstore.py:84-85).
verdicts.mutation_run: In my worktree I reverted the idempotency_key hunk (envelope.py:105-106) back to `raw = f"{ev.sender}:{ev.kind}:{subj}:{payload_digest(ev)}"` (dropping the revokes/supersedes suffix). Re-ran the 3 pins: ALL RED -> test_distinct_revoke_target_is_not_deduped FAILED, test_distinct_supersede_target_is_not_deduped FAILED, test_idempotency_key_distinguishes_revoke_and_supersede_target FAILED (assert idempotency_key(a) != idempotency_key(b) -> both '96898a92...'). Restored -> 3 passed. (Separately reverted ONLY the _request_fingerprint hunk with idempotency_key fixed -> pins stayed GREEN, see rule13_siblings / NIT: fingerprint branch never reached once idempotency_key separates targets.)
verdicts.rule13_siblings: Checked all request-content dedup / seen-set / CAS sites. (1) Remote push-CAS path: NOT a separate keying path — append() computes idempotency_key+_request_fingerprint once at refstore.py:133-134 and the `if self._remote` branch (refstore.py:172) is only the CAS WRITE step, so remote shares the fixed keys. Covered. (2) Gate seen_ids (gate.py:108) keys on `id` ALONE by design (ADR-037 collision/replay rejection); a distinct-target revoke has a distinct id so it is NOT suppressed -> different mechanism, not a twin. (3) Slice-1 store.py has NO request-content dedup, only an id-collision guard (store.py:43) -> bug cannot manifest, not a twin. (4) cutover/cursor_backfill/divergence `seen_dir` are per-SEAT cursor stores (last-seq), orthogonal to revoke targets. (5) legacy_projector.py:81 idempotency_key is comment-only (filenames unique). No uncovered twin.

issues.description: The _request_fingerprint half of the fix (refstore.py:84-85) is redundant given the idempotency_key fix and has NO independent red->green pin. Verified by mutation: reverting ONLY the fingerprint hunk leaves all 3 pins GREEN, because once idempotency_key (refstore.py:117) distinguishes distinct revoke/supersede targets, _find_verified_idempotent returns None and the fingerprint-comparison branch (refstore.py:140) is never reached for distinct targets. The addition is correct defense-in-depth (keeps the two keys consistent, protects a hypothetical future where idempotency_key is narrowed), but it is unpinned dead-relative-to-tests safety. Not a correctness hole.

None-inert comment (envelope.py:103-104: 'those events' dedup identity is unchanged') is true OPERATIONALLY but not byte-identically: the fix appends '::' (two empty-separator colons) to every non-revoke event's raw key, so the fixed hash != the pre-fix hash. Verified harmless: keys are recomputed fresh every scan (refstore.py:117) and never compared against any persisted old-formula key, so the uniform global rekey preserves distinct-stays-distinct / identical-stays-identical among non-revoke events (directly checked). Wording could note it is a uniform rekey, not a no-op hash.

verdicts.evidence: READ: envelope.py:53-54 (revokes/supersedes_event_id declared), :67-69 (excluded from signed/payload), :97-107 (idempotency_key fixed); refstore.py:73-87 (_request_fingerprint fixed), :105-124 (_find_verified_idempotent recomputes key fresh), :132-182 (append: keys computed once, remote branch only at CAS write :172-175); gate.py:99-110 (seen_ids keys on id alone, ADR-037); store.py:39-51 (Slice-1, id-collision guard only). RAN (abs venv path, env -u GIT_INDEX_FILE): 3 dedup pins -> `3 passed in 0.87s` on clean worktree. MUTATION (idempotency_key reverted) -> all 3 RED (IdempotencyKeyReused raised + idempotency_key(a)==idempotency_key(b) '96898a92...'); restored -> 3 passed. MUTATION (_request_fingerprint-only reverted) -> 3 still GREEN (proves fingerprint half unpinned). None-inert direct check: distinct non-revoke events still distinct=True, identical still same key=True. Regression: full test_threeway_refstore.py + test_threeway_cutover.py -> `34 passed in 28.18s`. Final clean-worktree pin run -> `3 passed`. git status --short on both files: empty (worktree restored to d3a157e1).

verdicts.overscope_or_regression: No over-narrowing, no regression. The diff touches exactly 8 files (3 source + 2 test + DECISIONS.md + inventory + audit artifact) and stays inside the two confirmed defects. Decision 1 only ADDS recovery robustness: best-effort-per-ref restores >= as many refs as the bare loop ever did, and chaining (raise TeardownError(...) from original) preserves the original cause — it never hides it. The happy-path teardown contract is unchanged: test_teardown_restores_preexisting_bus_on_failure_under_force and test_teardown_clean_restore_stays_silent both stay green, and both _teardown call sites (cutover.py:137 append-failure, :154 cursor-backfill-failure) were updated symmetrically; no third teardown path exists. Decision 2 only NARROWS dedup (more distinct requests land, never fewer) and is None-inert for all non-revoke/supersede events — verified the idempotency_key is a DERIVED field always recomputed and never read back by from_json_obj (envelope.py:133-135, refstore.py:117/:133), so no at-rest key is invalidated and the change is safe even mid-flight. The 14-field _signed_view and run_gate totality are untouched. Full threeway suite 328 passed / 0 failed (matches the commit claim), confirming no gate/reducer regression from the changed keys. Rule #13 sibling sweep: the COMPLETE set of dedup/identity surfaces in append's effectively-once path is {idempotency_key (envelope.py:97), _request_fingerprint (refstore.py:73)} — both fixed; the legacy store.py EventStore.append has NO idempotency/fingerprint dedup (only an id-collision raise) so it cannot carry the hole; _signed_view is correctly NOT widened (ref ids unsigned by design, ADR-036, crypto-binding deferred to scope-(b)).
verdicts.audit_findings_addressed: Both fixes match the audit wf_ce0bba9f-0ac CONFIRMED findings exactly. The audit confirmed precisely 2 MAJOR defects with recommended_rows row_id=cutover-teardown-nonatomic-masks-cause and row_id=refstore-dedup-omits-revoke-supersede-target; the two code fixes are 1:1 with those, with the audit's prescribed remedies (best-effort-per-ref + aggregate-chained-from-original; include both ref ids in BOTH keys). Non-vacuity PROVEN by mutation in my worktree: (1) reverting cutover.py to the pre-fix bare loop reddens test_teardown_best_effort_continues_and_chains_original_cause — the raw subprocess.CalledProcessError(128) propagates instead of a TeardownError chained from original, exactly the audit's reproduced harm (loop abort + masked cause); (2) reverting only the idempotency_key hunk reddens all 3 refstore pins (test_distinct_revoke_target_is_not_deduped raises IdempotencyKeyReused, the other two assert key inequality) — confirming idempotency_key is the load-bearing first layer; (3) reverting only the _request_fingerprint hunk leaves all 3 pins GREEN, validating the ADR's honestly-disclosed claim that the fingerprint half is defense-in-depth NOT independently reddenable (once idempotency_key distinguishes the target, the refstore.py:142 collision path is never reached). All 6 pins (5 new + the must-stay-green one) pass at HEAD. The audit's 7 preconditions_for_flip were NOT silently dropped — ADR-044's Flip-readiness paragraph preserves them verbatim as OPERATIONAL ('still stand and are NOT code': freeze tree, clean divergence check, force=True dry-run, out-of-band ref snapshot, chief/overseer key provisioning), correctly adds 'belt-and-suspenders now that teardown is best-effort' WITHOUT claiming the code fix obviates the operational freeze, keeps the live flip GATED on explicit user confirmation, and the 7th precondition (dedup follow-up) is now satisfied by the fix itself.
verdicts.doc_accuracy: Accurate. ADR-044 in DECISIONS.md describes the actual diff faithfully: Decision 1's best-effort + TeardownError-chained-from-original matches cutover.py:78-104 and the two updated callers; Decision 2's both-ids-in-both-keys matches envelope.py:105-106 and refstore.py:84-85; the None-inert + same-target-retry-still-dedups claims are correct (both fields default None, present-only as fields outside payload); the two-layer/defense-in-depth note ('once idempotency_key distinguishes the target, the fingerprint path is not reached — not independently reddenable, same shape as ADR-043') is empirically confirmed by my fingerprint-only mutation staying green; the verification block's '5 new tests RED pre-fix, 328 passed/0, adversarial Lane V pending' matches my run (328 passed). The two REMEDIATION-INVENTORY.md rows (threeway-cutover-teardown-nonatomic-masks-cause, threeway-refstore-dedup-omits-revoke-supersede-target) carry MAJOR severity, the correct file:line anchors (cutover.py:71-84; envelope.py:97-101 + refstore.py:73-83), repro_status yes, the exact pin names, status=fixed, verification='pending adversarial Lane V' (honest — not over-claiming verified), and 'Fixed via ADR-044' notes that match the code. Both rows correctly cite the audit artifact and the 2/3-refuters-failed-to-refute provenance.

summary.rationale: Both defect fixes are CORRECT and their pins are NON-VACUOUS, proven by 5 isolating mutations I ran in my own worktree (/Users/hyungkoookkim/Content/.claude/worktrees/lanev-adr044, since removed), each reverted after; worktree confirmed byte-clean vs d3a157e1 (git diff --stat exit 0) before and after each. DEFECT 1 (cutover.py:78-104 _teardown): M1 pre-fix bare loop (EVENTS-first, check=True, no try/chain) -> test_teardown_best_effort_continues_and_chains_original_cause RED (raw CalledProcessError(128) propagated on the events ref, loop aborted before the cursor ref, original masked). M2 drop only `from original` -> same pin RED at `assert ei.value.__cause__ is original` (None is RuntimeError) -> chaining independently load-bearing. M3 `if failures:`->`if True:` -> BOTH test_teardown_clean_restore_stays_silent AND the must-stay-green test_teardown_restores_preexisting_bus_on_failure_under_force RED (always-raise masked the injected cause). Both run_cutover call sites (cutover.py:137,154) updated symmetrically to `except Exception as original` + pass original; exactly two _teardown sites, no third path. DEFECT 2 (envelope.py:105-106 idempotency_key + refstore.py:84-85 _request_fingerprint): M4 revert only idempotency_key -> all 3 dedup pins RED (IdempotencyKeyReused + idempotency_key(a)==idempotency_key(b) '96898a92...'). M5 revert only _request_fingerprint (idempotency_key intact) -> all 3 pins GREEN, confirming the fingerprint half is honest, disclosed defense-in-depth (unreachable for distinct targets via _find_verified_idempotent returning None at refstore.py:138/140), NOT independently reddenable — not a correctness hole. Independent Rule #13 sweep (my own grep+read): complete request-content dedup surface = {idempotency_key, _request_fingerprint}, both fixed; gate.py:108 seen_ids keys on id alone (ADR-037, distinct id => not suppressed, not a twin); store.py:39-44 Slice-1 has only an id-collision filename glob, no request-content dedup (bug cannot manifest, not a twin); cursor_backfill.restore_from_manifest is a filesystem rollback, not a git-ref teardown twin; _signed_view (envelope.py:73-90) correctly NOT widened (revokes/supersedes intentionally unsigned, envelope.py:67-72). Regression: full threeway suite 328 passed/0 (matches commit claim), 34/34 cutover+refstore, 6 named pins green at baseline. The fix is correct and shippable; the two inventory rows may move fixed->verified. NITS (not FAIL, not pure GO) because of two minor, non-blocking observations below — neither is a hole in THIS commit's fix.
